title: District Cleanups
description: We clean up the streets of DC to make it a better place to live.  Check their calendar for upcoming events.

title: The Ballston Runaways
description: A running group in Ballston.  Meets in front of the Compass Coffee in Ballston.  Check the intagram for more details

schedules.location: Compass Coffee, Ballston, VA
Compass Coffee, Ballston, VA

title: DC City Girls
description: DC’s free social club for women.  They have some coed events as well.  Check the website for more details

title: Clarendon Run Club
description: A run club in clarendon.  Meets in front of Tatte (2805 Clarendon Blvd)

schedules.location: 2800 Clarendon Blvd, Arlington, Virginia 22201

title: North East Track Club
description: At NETC, new, amateur, veteran, and elite runners share a space, cross the same paths, and celebrate each others wins. We push and pull in different directions to make meaningful change through community, camaraderie, and service to our city.

schedules.time: Start time is 8 am during the summer months and 9 or 10 am during other parts of the year

title: District Running Collective
description: District Running Collective We proudly represent Washington, DC through each mile Weekly runs - Wednesdays at 6:30PM, Thursdays at 6:30AM, Saturdays at 8:30 AM

title: Dudes In The District
description: Dudes In The District is a group of men who meet up for social events in DC.  Check the website for more details

title: Washington Area Frisbee Club
description: The Washington Area Frisbee Club is a non-profit organization that promotes the sport of ultimate frisbee in the Washington, DC area.  They have a variety of leagues and events for all ages and skill levels.

title: Labrynth Board Game Night
description: A group of people who meet up to play board games.  Check the website for more details Thursday is open gaming night here at Labyrinth! Registration is not required but seating is limited. To make the most of our available seating this event will be on a first come, first served basis.

title: Washington Area Roadskaters
description: The Washington Area Roadskaters (WAR) is a non-profit organization whose mission is to promote safe and enjoyable skating in the Washington, D.C. area. WAR was founded in 1987 as Washington Area Rollerskaters and is one of the oldest skate clubs in the US.  Thousands of individuals have joined together to safely enjoy inline and roller skating in the streets of our fine city.

title: NOMA Run Club
venue: Check website for location.  Each day meets at a different location
description: NOMA Run Club is a social running club that meets every Monday and Thursday at 6:30 PM and Saturday at 8:00 AM. They offer a variety of runs, from easy to challenging, and more! (check the wesbite for more details)

title: Volo Sports
description: Volo Sports is a social sports club that offers a wide range of sports activities for adults. They offer basketball, volleyball, and soccer and more! (check the wesbite for more details)  They also offer a variety of social events, such as happy hours and game nights.

title: DC Fray
description: DC Fray is a social sports club that offers a wide range of sports activities for adults. They offer basketball, volleyball, and soccer and more! (check the wesbite for more details)  They also offer a variety of social events, such as happy hours and game nights.

title: Politics and Prose Book Clubs
description: Check the website for more information. Politics and Prose is a book club that meets at the Politics and Prose bookstore in Washington, D.C. The club is open to all and is free to join. The club meets on the first Tuesday of the month at 7:00 PM.

title: Time Left DC
description: Time Left is a way to meet new people in DC.  Book your seat now and meet 5 strangers over dinner, all matched by our personality algorithm.

title: DC Singles Meetup
description: A meetup group for singles in DC.  Check the website for more details

title: DC Swoon Speed Dating
description: Cityswoon DC: it's speed dating's cooler, more adventurous cousin.  Check the website for more details

title: Shuffle Dating
description: Speed dating but done through an app.  Check the website for more details

title: Pitch A Friend
description: Pitch A Friend is a social event that allows you to meet new people in a fun and interactive way.  Pitching your friend power-point stytle.  The event is free to attend and $5 to sign up to pitch your friend.  Check the website for more details

title: DC Road Runners
description: Running the nation's capital since 1961. DC Road Runners welcomes runners of all ability levels with a year-round schedule of events, weekly training runs, training programs, and 19 free races each year.

title: DC Front Runners
description: An LGBTQ+ running and walking club that holds a regular schedule of non-competitive group runs along Rock Creek Park, the National Mall, and the Potomac River banks. Choose your distance and set your pace. All are welcome.

schedules.location: Rock Creek Park, Washington, DC

title: DC Capital Striders
description: A community of runners in Washington, D.C., Northern Virginia, and Maryland that believes in running in a supportive, non-competitive environment. Great for beginners and experienced runners alike.

title: Capital Area Runners
description: A DC metropolitan area running club founded to provide group training opportunities for highly motivated and competitive minded runners of all ability levels.

title: Capital Hiking Club
description: A non-profit hiking group in the DC/MD/VA area operating since 1937. They hike 52 weeks a year with options that challenge all skill levels. One of the oldest and most active hiking clubs in the region.

schedules.location: Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA
Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA

title: Wanderbirds Hiking Club
description: Founded in 1934, the Wanderbirds is a non-profit, all-volunteer club that organizes day hikes every Sunday, year-round, for energetic folks in the DC area. Hikers range in age from 7 to over 80, and the club welcomes fast, experienced hikers as well as slower or beginning hikers.

schedules.location: Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA

title: Potomac Pedalers Touring Club
description: One of the largest bicycle clubs in the US. A non-profit educational, recreational and social organization for bicycling enthusiasts in the DC metro area with rides for all skill levels.

title: DC Paceline
description: One of Greater Washington Area's most well-known cycling training series. Four groups (A, B, C, and Juniors) ride criterium-style on a non-technical course. Great for community cycling rides in DC, Maryland, and Virginia.

title: Sends with Friends
description: A minority-led rock climbing community in the DMV area that brings together climbers of all cultural backgrounds and nationalities since 2021. Meets monthly on the first Friday at DC Bouldering Project.

schedules.location: DC Bouldering Project, 1200 Half St SE, Washington, DC 20003

title: Adventuring LGBTQ+
description: An outdoors club for the LGBTQ+ community in the Washington DC metro area. LGBTQ+ volunteers lead hikes, kayaking trips, camping, and other outdoor activities year-round. All experience levels welcome.

title: Baila DC
description: A premier salsa dance school in the DC metro area offering On2 salsa classes at 5 levels. Hosts two monthly 'Baila Thursdays' socials at La Cosecha in Union Market. No partner needed.

title: Salsa With Silvia
description: Fun and affordable Salsa, Bachata, and Reggaeton group classes in Washington, D.C. and Bethesda. Open to all levels from beginner to advanced with a welcoming social dance community.

title: DC Street Photography Collective
description: A non-profit 501(c)(3) dedicated to street photographers from the DC, MD, VA areas. Devoted to producing, sharing, and creatively capturing the human condition through photography walks and exhibits.

title: Exposed DC
description: Cultivates the Washington DC area photography community through news, highlighted images, regular social meetups, teach-ins, and an annual photography contest and exhibit. Open to photographers of all levels.

title: Solid State Books Book Clubs
description: DC's independent bookstore at Union Market hosts multiple book clubs including a Horror book club (3rd Wednesday monthly) and a Science-Fiction & Fantasy book club. A welcoming community for readers of all genres.

schedules.location: Solid State Books, 600 H St NE, Washington, DC 20002

title: ZogSports DC
description: Sign up as a whole team or register individually for coed recreational sports leagues including volleyball, kickball, dodgeball, softball, and more. Leagues end with happy hours and social events.

title: OutLoud Sports DC
description: A leading LGBTQ+ recreational sports organization in Washington DC that promotes inclusivity and fun through diverse sports including sand volleyball, tennis, pickleball, and dodgeball. All are welcome regardless of identity.

title: DC Pickleball League
description: Multi-week ladder leagues with both social and competitive divisions at Rock Creek and WTEF. Great for meeting fellow pickleball players in the DC area at all skill levels.

title: Free Yoga For Everyone DC
description: A Meetup group with over 1,000 members that practices traditional Ashtanga yoga together completely free of charge in open areas and parks in DC and Northern Virginia. All levels welcome, no experience needed.

title: Spanglish Exchange DC
description: A language exchange that brings together native English speakers and native Spanish speakers in a relaxed social setting. Practice conversation skills while making new friends over drinks.

title: DC Language and Cultural Exchange Club
description: A social group where people get together to practice and learn new languages with weekly language exchange meetings. Mainly a social for intermediate, advanced, and native speakers of many languages.

title: Washington Area Bicyclist Association
description: WABA advocates for better biking in the DC region and organizes community rides, bike-to-work events, and cycling education classes. They also lead group rides and social cycling events throughout the year.

title: Take a Hike DMV
description: A friendly and inclusive group for anyone who loves exploring the outdoors in the DC, Maryland, and Virginia area. They organize hikes across the DMV focusing on scenic and accessible trails within an easy drive of DC.

title: Pour House Trivia
description: One of the largest trivia companies in the DC region with over 800 teams participating each week at dozens of bars and restaurants. Free to play with gift card prizes. Check their website for the full venue schedule.

title: Soul Trak Outdoors
description: A DC-based nonprofit organization that connects communities of color to outdoor spaces through hiking, climbing, and nature experiences. Meets monthly on the third Sunday at DC Bouldering Project.

schedules.location: DC Bouldering Project, 1200 Half St SE, Washington, DC 20003

title: Pacers Running
description: DC's flagship run shop hosts free group runs from multiple locations across the DMV. With runs departing from Georgetown, 14th Street, Navy Yard, Arlington, and Alexandria, there's always a Pacers run near you. All paces welcome.

schedules.location: Various Pacers locations (Georgetown, 14th St, Navy Yard, Arlington, Alexandria)
Various Pacers locations
Various Pacers locations

title: November Project DC
description: A free fitness movement that meets rain or shine for intense outdoor workouts three days a week. Mondays at Malcolm X Park, Wednesdays at the Lincoln Memorial, and Fridays at rotating locations. Show up, work hard, and join one of the most welcoming fitness communities in DC.

schedules.location: Joan of Arc Statue, Malcolm X Park, Washington, DC
Reflecting Pool Steps, Lincoln Memorial, Washington, DC
Rotating — check Instagram for details

title: Georgetown Running Company Run Club
description: Weekly group run departing from the Georgetown Running Company store. A casual, social run through Georgetown and along the C&O Canal towpath. All paces welcome, with post-run socializing at nearby spots.

schedules.location: Georgetown Running Company, 3401 M St NW, Washington, DC

title: Lululemon Run Clubs DC
description: Free weekly run clubs hosted by various Lululemon stores across the DC area. Each store offers its own schedule and route. Great for meeting fellow runners in a social, low-pressure environment.

title: Heartbreak Run Club
description: A vibrant DC-area run club with a strong social media community. Known for themed runs, post-run socials, and an energetic atmosphere. Follow their Instagram for weekly meetup details and locations.

title: Bethesda Running Club
description: A community running club based in Bethesda, Maryland serving runners of all abilities. Organizes group runs, training programs, and social events in the Bethesda and greater Montgomery County area.

title: East City Bookshop Book Club
description: Capitol Hill's beloved independent bookstore hosts a monthly book club open to all readers. Selections span fiction, nonfiction, and local authors. Check their website for the current pick and meeting dates.

title: Loyalty Bookstores Book Club
description: An independent bookstore in Petworth and Silver Spring that centers diverse voices. Their monthly book club features works by authors of color and from marginalized communities. Welcoming space for all readers.

title: Lost City Books Book Club
description: An independent used bookstore in Adams Morgan that hosts a monthly book club. Known for eclectic selections and a cozy, community-driven atmosphere. Check their events page for upcoming meetings.

title: Capitol Hill Books Reading Groups
description: The beloved, floor-to-ceiling packed used bookstore on Capitol Hill organizes reading groups for book lovers. A DC literary institution since 1991. Check their website for current group schedules.

title: Kramers Book Clubs
description: The iconic Dupont Circle bookstore-café hosts multiple book clubs covering fiction, nonfiction, and more. A DC literary landmark since 1976 with a vibrant community of readers. Check their events calendar for details.

title: New Columbia Swing
description: DC's home for swing dancing with Friday and Saturday evening dances featuring live bands and DJs. Beginner lessons included before each dance. No partner or experience needed — just come ready to have fun.

schedules.location: Check website for venue
Check website for venue

title: Friday Night Dancers DC
description: A free outdoor social dance gathering on Friday evenings in DC. Styles range from salsa and bachata to swing and freestyle. A welcoming, open-air dance community — no partner needed.

schedules.time: Evening — check social media for times
schedules.location: Outdoor locations in DC — check social media

title: Glen Echo Park Partner Dancing
description: Historic Glen Echo Park hosts partner dancing Friday through Sunday evenings including swing, contra, Cajun, and ballroom. The iconic Spanish Ballroom and Bumper Car Pavilion provide a magical setting. Beginner lessons often included.

schedules.location: Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD
Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD
Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD

title: DC Gay Flag Football League
description: One of the largest LGBTQ+ flag football leagues in the country. Seasonal play on Saturdays with multiple skill divisions. A welcoming, competitive, and social community for all experience levels.

schedules.location: Various DC fields

title: Stonewall Sports DC
description: An LGBTQ+ and ally social sports league offering seasonal leagues in kickball, dodgeball, bocce, volleyball, and more. Focused on community, fun, and inclusivity with post-game socials at local bars.

title: Stonewall Kickball DC
description: DC's premier LGBTQ+ kickball league plays on Sundays during the season. Known for its spirited post-game bar parties and strong community bonds. All skill levels welcome — the emphasis is on fun and inclusivity.

schedules.location: Various DC fields

title: Team DC
description: An umbrella organization supporting LGBTQ+ sports teams and athletes in the DC metro area. Connects you to dozens of LGBTQ+-friendly sports leagues and teams across every sport imaginable.

title: Gay for Good DC
description: A volunteer organization that mobilizes the LGBTQ+ community and allies for monthly service projects across the DC area. Projects range from park cleanups to food bank shifts. A great way to give back and meet people.

schedules.location: Various locations in DC

title: Gay Men's Chorus of Washington
description: One of the premier LGBTQ+ choruses in the country, performing choral music since 1981. Rehearsals on Tuesday evenings. Open to singers of all skill levels who identify as male. Multiple performances throughout the year.

schedules.location: Check website for rehearsal location

title: The DC Center
description: The DC area's community center for the LGBTQ+ community offering daily drop-in hours, support groups, social events, and resources. A welcoming space for everyone in the LGBTQ+ community and allies.

title: FXA Sports
description: Formerly known as Federal Sports, FXA offers coed and single-gender recreational sports leagues across DC, Maryland, and Virginia. Sports include soccer, flag football, softball, volleyball, and more with post-game socials.

title: District Soccer
description: Adult recreational soccer leagues in Washington, DC with divisions for all skill levels. Seasonal play with both coed and single-gender options. A fun way to stay active and meet fellow soccer fans in the city.

title: WATA Tennis
description: The Washington Area Tennis Association organizes seasonal tennis leagues, tournaments, and social events for adult players across the DC metro area. Divisions from beginner to advanced.

title: Rogue Cornhole DC
description: A social cornhole league in DC that combines competition with community. Weekly games at local bars and venues with post-game socializing. No experience needed — teams of all skill levels welcome.

title: Play Play DC
description: A social sports and events company in DC offering a variety of recreational leagues, tournaments, and social events. Sports include volleyball, kickball, soccer, and more. Focused on fun and meeting new people.

title: Capital Rowing Club
description: A community rowing club on the Potomac River open to adults of all experience levels. Offers learn-to-row programs, competitive teams, and recreational rowing. Morning practices with stunning views of the DC monuments.

schedules.location: Anacostia River, Washington, DC

title: Potomac Boat Club
description: One of DC's oldest athletic organizations, founded in 1859. Located on the Georgetown waterfront, the club offers rowing, paddling, and social events. A historic institution on the Potomac River.

title: Sierra Club Potomac Region
description: The local chapter of the nation's largest grassroots environmental organization. Organizes weekend hikes, nature outings, conservation volunteer events, and educational programs throughout the DC, Maryland, and Virginia region.

schedules.location: Various trails in DC, MD, VA

title: Potomac Appalachian Trail Club
description: PATC maintains over 1,000 miles of trails including 240 miles of the Appalachian Trail. Organizes hiking, backpacking, trail maintenance, and cabin trips. One of the most active outdoor clubs in the Mid-Atlantic since 1927.

schedules.location: Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA, WV

title: Washington Women Outdoors
description: A nonprofit outdoor adventure club for women in the DC area offering hiking, backpacking, kayaking, skiing, and more. Trips range from beginner-friendly day hikes to challenging multi-day backcountry adventures.

title: Potomac Mountain Club
description: A DC-area outdoor recreation club focused on rock climbing, hiking, and mountaineering. Organizes weekend climbing trips, hiking outings, and climbing gym meetups. Welcomes all skill levels from beginners to experienced climbers.

title: Mid-Atlantic Off-Road Enthusiasts
description: MORE builds and maintains mountain bike trails throughout the DC metro area and organizes group rides for all skill levels. One of the largest mountain biking clubs on the East Coast with trails in Maryland and Virginia.

title: Streets Calling Bike Club DC
description: A community cycling group in DC that organizes weekly social rides through the city. Open to all riders and bike types. A welcoming crew focused on exploring DC neighborhoods together on two wheels.

title: DC Bike Party
description: A free, monthly group bike ride through the streets of DC on the second Friday of every month. Hundreds of riders cruise together at a leisurely pace with music and good vibes. All bikes and riders welcome.

schedules.location: Check social media for start location

title: District Cycling Collective
description: A community-driven cycling group in DC that organizes weekly rides, advocacy events, and social gatherings for cyclists of all levels. Focused on making cycling more accessible and fun in the District.

title: One Brick DC
description: A volunteer network that makes it easy to give back without a long-term commitment. Monthly volunteer events across DC ranging from park cleanups to food bank shifts. Meet like-minded people while making a difference.

title: GatherDC
description: A community organization that brings DC residents together through social events, volunteer opportunities, and neighborhood meetups. Regular happy hours, game nights, and community service projects.

title: City Blossoms
description: A DC-based nonprofit that creates kid-driven green spaces in under-resourced neighborhoods. Volunteer opportunities include garden builds, planting days, and youth program support. Seasonal events throughout the year.

title: Cultivate the City
description: An urban farming nonprofit in DC that operates rooftop farms and community gardens. Volunteer to help grow fresh produce for the community. Seasonal volunteer days and workshops throughout the growing season.

title: DC Social Collective
description: A social group that organizes events for young professionals in DC including happy hours, brunches, fitness classes, and cultural outings. A great way to expand your social circle in the District.

title: Pasta Social Club
description: A supper club that brings strangers together over homemade pasta in DC. Monthly dinners at rotating venues where you meet new people while enjoying freshly made Italian dishes. Tickets sell out fast.

title: Chess on Tap
description: A casual chess meetup at DC-area bars where players of all skill levels gather for games over drinks. No chess set needed — boards are provided. A fun, low-key way to sharpen your game and meet people.

title: Washington Improv Theater
description: DC's nonprofit improv theater offering classes, shows, and a welcoming community of performers. Take a class to learn improv comedy or catch a show any night of the week. No experience needed to start.

title: Capitol Hill Writers Group
description: A monthly gathering of writers on Capitol Hill sharing work and providing constructive feedback. Open to writers of all genres and experience levels — fiction, nonfiction, poetry, and more.

title: DC Writers' Salon
description: A supportive community for DC-area writers offering monthly meetups, workshops, and writing sessions. Whether you're working on a novel, screenplay, or blog, this group provides motivation and feedback.

title: Congressional Chorus
description: A premier community chorus in DC that brings together singers from across the metro area. Tuesday evening rehearsals with multiple concerts per season at iconic DC venues. Auditions held seasonally — all voice parts welcome.

schedules.location: Check website for rehearsal location

title: City Choir of Washington
description: A vibrant community choir open to singers of all backgrounds and skill levels. Weekly rehearsals and performances throughout the year featuring diverse choral repertoire. No audition required.

title: National Capital Astronomers
description: An amateur astronomy club serving the DC area since 1937. Monthly meetings with guest speakers, telescope viewings, and star parties at nearby dark-sky locations. Perfect for space enthusiasts of all levels.

title: National Capital Area Garden Clubs
description: A federation of garden clubs in the DC metro area organizing monthly meetings, garden tours, flower shows, and educational programs. Connect with fellow gardening enthusiasts across the region.
